steps.title: Use slope-intercept form
steps.content: A linear function can be written as $f(x)=mx+b$, where $m$ is the slope and $b=f(0)$.

steps.title: Substitute the values
steps.content: The slope is $-3$, so $m=-3$. The line passes through $(0,-17)$, so $f(0)=-17$ and $b=-17$. Therefore $$f(x)=-3x-17.$$

steps.title: Match the equation
steps.content: A choice with slope $-17$, not $-3$. A choice with the right slope but goes through $(0,0)$ instead of $(0,-17)$. A choice with positive slope. Only the matching choice, $f(x)=-3x-17$, matches. C is correct.
